mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions or produces negative returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal is neutralized by concurrent GDP growth above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when payroll growth is driven solely by temporary government hiring, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that improves corporate profit forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or GDP growth fails to correlate with short-term equity momentum during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in more than 55% of contraction instances, or the predictive power disappears when monetary policy is actively easing.
